scope: Pass B full-surface expansion. developer.ticktick.com/api is a JS-rendered, bot-walled page that returns HTTP 403 to automated fetches; ground truth for this review is TickTick's own published OpenAPI 3.0 document for this exact product (title 'TickTick OpenAPI', servers: https://ticktick.com, base path /open/v1 — matching this bundle's base_url exactly; mirrored at https://storage.googleapis.com/zapvine-prod.appspot.com/specs/ticktick.com.yaml), cross-checked against TickTick's help-center Open-API article (https://help.ticktick.com/articles/7055781495671095296). That OpenAPI document declares EXACTLY 6 operations across 4 paths, all accounted for below (2 read streams, 3 write actions, 1 duplicate-of exclusion). No update-task, create-project, update-project, or delete-project operation exists anywhere in the official Open API document; no tags/habits/columns/focus/pomodoro resource is exposed by it either — corroborated by a 71-tool third-party MCP server whose own README explicitly states it covers 'both the official TickTick Open API (V1) ... and the unofficial web API (V2) for features not yet available publicly', bucketing tags/habits/columns/folders/focus under its V2-only (unofficial) tool groups. TickTick's own public Open API is genuinely this narrow, not under-researched here. A handful of secondary community references (e.g. a third-party API-gateway skill README) additionally describe project-create/update/delete and task-GET/update operations reachable at the same base URL; none of these could be corroborated against the official OpenAPI document or TickTick's help-center pages, so they are excluded below as unconfirmed rather than risking a fabricated write shape against a real user's account.

excluded.reason: single-project detail returns a strict subset of the fields already present on every 'projects' stream record sharing the same id (identical Project schema); adding it as its own stream would duplicate already-covered data

project creation appears only in unofficial community references, never in TickTick's own published Open API document or help-center Open-API article; not implemented without a corroborated real request/response shape

project deletion appears only in unofficial community references, never in TickTick's own published Open API document or help-center Open-API article; not implemented without a corroborated real request/response shape
task update (distinct from create/complete/delete) appears only in unofficial community references; TickTick's own published Open API document declares no update-task operation at all

single-task GET appears only in unofficial community references; TickTick's own published Open API document declares no single-task-GET operation, and the 'tasks' stream's project-data endpoint already returns every task's full record
tags are exposed only by TickTick's unofficial internal web API (V2), never by the public Open API (V1) OAuth surface this bundle authenticates against
habit tracking is exposed only by TickTick's unofficial internal web API (V2), never by the public Open API (V1) OAuth surface this bundle authenticates against

kanban column data is already returned inline on the 'tasks' stream's project-data response (the ProjectData.columns array); no separate documented endpoint to list columns independently of a project exists in the official Open API
focus/pomodoro statistics are exposed only by TickTick's unofficial internal web API (V2), never by the public Open API (V1) OAuth surface this bundle authenticates against
